title: English Infant Educarer
description:
Purpose of the Job

The primary goal of an Infant Educarer is to provide a loving, safe, and developmentally appropriate environment that promotes the well-being and early learning of infants aged 2 to 18 months. The Infant Educarer plays a critical role in offering nurturing care, supporting infants’ emotional and physical needs, and fostering their growth through intentional interactions and early learning experiences.

Job Description

Child Development & Observation:

  • Regularly observe, assess, and document each infant’s development and growth.
  • Identify and report any developmental concerns in a timely and professional manner.

Health, Safety & Supervision:

  • Ensure the safety and security of each child through constant supervision, whether in the classroom, play areas, or other parts of the centre.
  • Adhere strictly to health, hygiene, and safety protocolsset by ECDA and the centre.

Routine Care:

  • Carry out all infant care routines in a prompt, hygienic, and respectful manner, such as diapering, feeding (milk and solids), bathing, handwashing, nap time and transitions between activities.

Record Keeping:

  • Maintain an up-to-date daily journal for each child, documenting milk feeds, solid food intake, diapering, sleeping patterns, and other care routines.
  • Ensure records are clear, accurate, and relevant to each age group (mobile & non-mobile).

Curriculum & Learning Activities:

  • Plan and implement age-appropriate, engaging learning experiences that encourage exploration and holistic development.
  • Set up safe and stimulating environments that support cognitive, language, physical, and social-emotional growth.

Communication & Teamwork:

  • Communicate regularly with parents regarding their child’s routines and development.
  • Collaborate closely with colleagues to ensure consistent care practices and promote a supportive team environment.

Qualifications and Experience

  • Higher Certificate in Infant Care (HCIC) / Advanced Certificate in Early Years (ACEY).
  • With at least 1 year of relevant experience in Infant Care setting.

postedCompany.description:
Society of Sheng Hong Welfare Services (SSHWS) was set up as the welfare arm of Lorong Koo Chye Sheng Hong Temple Association with the objective of carrying out relevant good work and welfare activities to relieve hardship as well as to bring benefits to the society and the community.


Membership to SSHWS is open to all of age 18 and above, sharing the same objectives irrespective of race, colour, creed, religion, language, sex and social status.


SSHWS is governed by a Management Committee whose members are elected according to the governing instrument, which is the Constitution. All our members serve on a voluntary basis without receiving any form of remuneration.


SSHWS holds the status of Institution of Public Character (IPC) since 3 November 2000. With the IPC status, all outright cash donations are tax deductible.
postedCompany.name: Society of Sheng Hong Welfare Services
